epics.title: Plugin System Core
epics.description: Plugin architecture for NumenText using embedded Lua (gopher-lua) with extension points and plugin.json manifests. Plugins live in ~/.numentext/plugins/ and are installed from GitHub repos via git clone.

user_stories.title: Embedded Lua runtime via gopher-lua
user_stories.description: Integrate gopher-lua as an embedded Lua 5.1 interpreter. Create a sandbox with controlled API exposure. Plugins run in-process with access only to the NumenText API, not arbitrary Go packages or OS calls beyond what the host exposes.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Lua file, when loaded by the runtime, then it executes within the embedded interpreter
Given a plugin Lua script, when it attempts os.execute or io.open, then the call is blocked by the sandbox
Given the Lua runtime, when a plugin errors, then the error is caught and displayed in the output panel without crashing NumenText
Given multiple plugins, when loaded, then each runs in its own Lua state with isolated globals
Given the Lua runtime, when a plugin enters an infinite loop, then a timeout mechanism stops it after 5 seconds

user_stories.title: Plugin Host API exposed to Lua
user_stories.description: Expose the NumenText API to Lua plugins as a global 'numen' table. Includes file operations, status bar, output panel, editor queries, and event registration. This is the contract between plugins and the editor.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Lua plugin, when it calls numen.open_file('path'), then the editor opens that file
Given a Lua plugin, when it calls numen.status_message('text'), then the status bar shows the message
Given a Lua plugin, when it calls numen.output_append('text'), then text appears in the output panel
Given a Lua plugin, when it calls numen.active_file(), then it receives the current file path
Given a Lua plugin, when it calls numen.active_content(), then it receives the current buffer text
Given a Lua plugin, when it calls numen.cursor_position(), then it receives row and column
Given a Lua plugin, when it calls numen.set_cursor(row, col), then the cursor moves to that position
Given a Lua plugin, when it calls numen.insert_text('text'), then text is inserted at the cursor

user_stories.title: Extension points: commands, menus, keybindings
user_stories.description: Plugins register commands (for the command palette), menu items (in any menu), and keyboard shortcuts via the plugin.json manifest or at runtime via Lua calls. Commands are callable from the palette, menus, or shortcuts.
acceptance_criteria: Given a plugin.json with a command 'myplugin.doThing', when the command palette opens, then the command appears in the list
Given a plugin.json with a menu item under 'Tools', when the Tools menu opens, then the item appears
Given a plugin.json with a shortcut 'Ctrl+Shift+J', when pressed, then the registered Lua function is called
Given a Lua plugin calling numen.register_command('name', 'title', func), when registered, then the command is available in the palette
Given a command is invoked, when the Lua function executes, then it has access to the full numen API
Given a plugin is unloaded, when its commands are removed, then they disappear from the palette and menus

user_stories.title: Extension points: file type handlers and panels
user_stories.description: Plugins can claim file types (e.g., .ipynb) and provide custom rendering/editing. Plugins can register panels (side, bottom, or editor-area) as tview primitives created from Lua or as custom Draw functions.
acceptance_criteria: Given a plugin claiming '.ipynb' in plugin.json, when a .ipynb file is opened, then the plugin's handler is called instead of the default editor
Given a plugin registering a panel, when numen.register_panel('name', 'bottom') is called, then a panel area is allocated
Given a plugin panel, when the plugin calls numen.panel_set_content('name', 'text'), then the panel displays the text
Given a plugin panel, when the plugin calls numen.panel_append('name', 'line'), then the line is appended
Given a file type claimed by a plugin, when the file is opened, then the plugin receives the file content and path
Given a plugin panel, when it is toggled via its registered shortcut, then it shows or hides

user_stories.title: Extension points: event listeners
user_stories.description: Plugins can subscribe to editor events: file open, file save, file close, buffer change, cursor move, theme change. Events are dispatched to all subscribed plugins.
acceptance_criteria: Given a plugin calling numen.on('file_open', func), when a file is opened, then the callback receives the file path
Given a plugin calling numen.on('file_save', func), when a file is saved, then the callback receives the file path
Given a plugin calling numen.on('file_close', func), when a tab is closed, then the callback receives the file path
Given a plugin calling numen.on('cursor_move', func), when the cursor moves, then the callback receives row and col
Given a plugin calling numen.on('theme_change', func), when the theme switches, then the callback receives the theme name
Given multiple plugins subscribing to the same event, when the event fires, then all callbacks are called in registration order

user_stories.title: Plugin manifest and loader
user_stories.description: On startup, scan ~/.numentext/plugins/ for directories containing plugin.json. Parse manifests, validate, and load init.lua for each enabled plugin. Support enable/disable per plugin in config. Show loaded plugins in Options menu.
acceptance_criteria: Given ~/.numentext/plugins/jupyter/plugin.json exists, when NumenText starts, then the plugin manifest is parsed
Given a valid manifest and init.lua, when the plugin loads, then init.lua is executed in the Lua runtime
Given a plugin with errors in init.lua, when loading fails, then the error is shown in the output panel and other plugins still load
Given a plugin is disabled in config, when NumenText starts, then that plugin is skipped
Given Options > Plugins menu, when opened, then all discovered plugins are listed with enabled/disabled status
Given a plugin.json with min_version higher than current NumenText, when loading, then the plugin is skipped with a version warning

user_stories.title: Plugin install, update, and remove via CLI
user_stories.description: Support --plugin-install, --plugin-update, --plugin-remove, and --plugin-list CLI flags. Install clones a GitHub repo to ~/.numentext/plugins/. Update runs git pull. Remove deletes the directory. List shows installed plugins with versions.
acceptance_criteria: Given 'numentext --plugin-install github.com/user/numentext-myplugin', when executed, then the repo is cloned to ~/.numentext/plugins/myplugin/
Given an installed plugin, when 'numentext --plugin-update myplugin' is executed, then git pull runs in the plugin directory
Given an installed plugin, when 'numentext --plugin-remove myplugin' is executed, then the plugin directory is deleted
Given 'numentext --plugin-list', when executed, then all installed plugins are listed with name, version, and enabled status
Given a non-existent GitHub repo, when install is attempted, then an error message is shown
Given 'numentext --plugin-update' with no argument, when executed, then all installed plugins are updated

user_stories.title: Plugin registry index for discovery
user_stories.description: Support an optional plugin registry (a JSON file hosted on GitHub) that lists available plugins with metadata. NumenText can search the registry to discover plugins. The registry is a simple curated list, not a full marketplace.
acceptance_criteria: Given 'numentext --plugin-search jupyter', when executed, then the registry is fetched and matching plugins are listed
Given the registry URL is configurable in config.json, when set, then NumenText uses that URL for searches
Given the default registry, when fetched, then it returns a JSON array of plugin entries with name, repo, description, version
Given no network access, when search is attempted, then a timeout error is shown without hanging
Given a search result, when 'numentext --plugin-install' is used with the repo URL, then the plugin installs normally

epics.title: Jupyter Notebook Plugin
epics.description: A NumenText plugin for viewing, editing, and executing Jupyter notebooks (.ipynb). Claims the .ipynb file type and provides a cell-based viewer with code execution via the Jupyter REST API. First plugin built on the plugin system.

user_stories.title: Parse and render .ipynb files as cells
user_stories.description: Parse .ipynb JSON format into cells (code, markdown, raw). Render cells in a custom panel: code cells with syntax highlighting based on kernel language, markdown cells as rendered text (reusing markdown preview), output cells below their code cells. Cell boundaries shown with horizontal separators.
acceptance_criteria: Given a .ipynb file, when opened, then the notebook viewer shows all cells in order
Given a code cell with Python code, when rendered, then syntax highlighting is applied
Given a markdown cell, when rendered, then headers, bold, italic, code spans are styled (reusing markdown preview)
Given a cell with text output, when rendered, then the output appears below the code cell in a distinct style
Given cell boundaries, when rendered, then horizontal separators with cell type labels (Code/Markdown/Raw) divide cells
Given a notebook with 50+ cells, when scrolling, then performance is smooth

user_stories.title: Cell navigation and selection
user_stories.description: Navigate between cells with keyboard shortcuts. Select a cell to highlight it. Enter a cell to edit its content. Escape exits cell editing and returns to cell navigation mode.
acceptance_criteria: Given the notebook viewer, when Up/Down arrows are pressed in navigation mode, then the previous/next cell is selected
Given a selected cell, when Enter is pressed, then the cell enters edit mode with a cursor inside
Given a cell in edit mode, when Escape is pressed, then edit mode exits and returns to cell navigation
Given cell navigation mode, when 'a' is pressed, then a new code cell is inserted above the current cell
Given cell navigation mode, when 'b' is pressed, then a new code cell is inserted below the current cell
Given cell navigation mode, when 'dd' is pressed, then the current cell is deleted with undo support
Given cell navigation mode, when 'm' is pressed, then the cell type toggles between code and markdown

user_stories.title: Cell execution via Jupyter REST API
user_stories.description: Connect to a running Jupyter server's REST API. Execute code cells by sending content to a kernel. Display text output inline below the cell. Handle execution state (idle, busy, error). Support Shift+Enter to execute current cell and move to next.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Jupyter server running on localhost:8888, when a cell is executed with Shift+Enter, then the code is sent to the kernel
Given a cell execution, when the kernel returns stdout output, then it appears below the cell
Given a cell execution, when the kernel returns an error traceback, then it appears in red below the cell
Given no Jupyter server running, when execution is attempted, then the status bar shows 'No Jupyter server. Run: jupyter server'
Given a cell is executing, when the status bar updates, then it shows a busy indicator
Given Shift+Enter on the last cell, when executed, then a new empty code cell is created below
Given Ctrl+Shift+Enter, when pressed, then all cells execute in order from top to bottom

user_stories.title: Notebook save and kernel management
user_stories.description: Save modified notebooks back to .ipynb JSON format preserving all metadata. Manage kernel connections: auto-connect to running kernels, show kernel status, allow kernel restart/interrupt from the menu.
acceptance_criteria: Given a modified notebook, when Ctrl+S is pressed, then the notebook is saved in .ipynb JSON format with all cell outputs preserved
Given the saved file, when opened in Jupyter Lab, then it renders correctly with all outputs
Given a running kernel, when the kernel status is shown, then the status bar displays the kernel name and state (idle/busy)
Given the Tools menu in notebook mode, when opened, then it shows Kernel: Restart, Kernel: Interrupt, Kernel: Change options
Given Kernel: Restart, when selected, then the kernel restarts and all cell outputs are cleared
Given Kernel: Interrupt, when selected, then a running cell execution is interrupted
Given no kernel connected, when a notebook is opened, then NumenText attempts to start a kernel automatically via the Jupyter API

user_stories.title: Image output handling
user_stories.description: Handle image outputs from cells (matplotlib plots, PIL images). Save image data to temporary files and show the file path in the output area. On terminals that support Sixel or Kitty graphics protocol, render the image inline.
acceptance_criteria: Given a cell that produces a matplotlib plot, when executed, then the image is saved to a temp file and the path is shown below the cell
Given image output, when displayed, then the output shows '[Image: /tmp/numentext-plot-001.png]' with the file path
Given a terminal that supports Sixel protocol, when image output is available, then the image renders inline in the terminal
Given a terminal without graphics support, when image output is available, then only the file path is shown
Given multiple image outputs from one cell, when displayed, then each image gets a separate file and path
